athlete_profile: Age: 32, Sex: Male, Height: 178 cm, Weight: 82 kg, Running experience: 4 months. Beginner runner who started running in late 2025. No prior endurance sport background. Runs 2-3 times per week, mostly on flat urban routes. No known injuries but occasionally feels tightness in calves after runs. VO2max estimated at 38.5 mL/kg/min. Resting heart rate: 68 bpm. Average HRV: 42 ms.
user_message: I've been running for about 4 months now but I feel like I'm not improving much. My legs are often sore the day after running and my pace doesn't seem to get any faster. Should I run more often or change something in my training?
training_data:
Last 4 weeks summary:
- Week of Feb 23: 2 runs, 8.3 km total, avg pace 6:42/km, avg HR 159 bpm
- Week of Feb 16: 3 runs, 13.0 km total, avg pace 6:44/km, avg HR 162 bpm
- Week of Feb 9: 2 runs, 7.5 km total, avg pace 6:55/km, avg HR 165 bpm
- Week of Feb 2: 2 runs, 6.8 km total, avg pace 7:02/km, avg HR 164 bpm

Trends: Average heart rate is consistently above 155 bpm, suggesting most runs are at moderate-to-hard intensity. Pace is relatively flat around 6:30-7:00/km. Weekly volume is low and inconsistent (2-3 runs, 7-13 km/week). Longest single run: 6.0 km (41 min).
additional_context: Runner tends to run all sessions at the same moderate-hard effort. Heart rate data suggests no easy runs are truly easy (all above 155 bpm for a max HR estimated around 188 bpm). Sleep is slightly below recommended (6.8h average). Low HRV (42 ms) may indicate accumulated fatigue or insufficient recovery. Cadence is low (155-160 spm), suggesting potential overstriding.
plan_context: Goal type: race. Race distance: 10K. Race date: 2026-06-14. Available training weeks: approximately 15 weeks from today. The athlete is a beginner with 4 months of experience. Plan should start conservatively with run/walk progression and build toward continuous running, then 10K-specific preparation. 3 sessions per week recommended.
